text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland Richard Payne and Matt Norris The Ascension of the Lord May 17 2026 Welcome home to all of our college students and congrats to new graduates This weekend May 16th 17th the Church celebrates the Solemnity of the Ascension of the Lord Marking forty days since Easter Sunday and the last Sunday of Eastertide we reflect upon the great mystery of the Lord Jesus ascension through His own power to the right hand of the Father This celebration is not to be confused with that of the Assumption where Mary is taken up body and soul by the power of God As we enter the Liturgy of the Eucharist this weekend the Preface offered for celebration already provides a beautiful reflection for us to reflect upon The Lord Jesus ascended today to the highest heavens as the Angels gazed in wonder Mediator between God and man He ascended not to distance himself from our lowly state but that we his members might be confident of following where he our Head and Founder has gone before Even though Eastertide is coming to an end there is still a lot to celebrate After the Ascension this weekend we will then celebrate the Solemnity of Pentecost the Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity and finally the Feast of Corpus Christi over the next three weekends Following Corpus Christi and our annual Corpus Christi Procession after the 10 AM Mass on June 7th we will enter back into Ordinary Time for the remainder of the liturgical year Pentecost Collection for Seminarian Education Every registered parishioner household will receive a letter from Bishop Rhoades regarding our annual Pentecost Collection for the Education of Seminarians The Holy Spirit continues to call men from our diocese to prepare for the priesthood Through this collection we have the opportunity to support their formation and share in the mission of the Church Please pray for our seminarians and for all those discerning God's call Memorial Day Mass You are invited to join us this Memorial Day Monday May 25th at 10 AM for a special Mass at St Joseph Catholic Cemetery on SR 124 as we pray for all those who have died in service to our country and for all the faithful departed In the midst of remembrance and gratitude we commend those who have given their lives for our freedoms to the mercy of God and place our hope in the Resurrection of Jesus Christ Parish Council Our Parish Council held its final meeting for the year at the beginning of May We will take our annual break over the coming summer months before we resume our monthly meetings in August We extend a heartfelt Thank you to Lori Bender and Tyler Hoffacker as their three year term on the Parish Council ends Their leadership and dedication to our parish have been deeply appreciated Nominations to fill these two vacancies will open in June Graduation Mass On Sunday June 14th at the 10 AM Mass we will celebrate our annual Graduate's Mass for our graduating High School Seniors If you have a High School Senior who is graduating and would like them to be recognized at this Mass please contact the Parish Office for more information General Update Parish Renovations It was slow going on the construction side of renovations this week We've received confirmation regarding shipments for our various flooring material At the same time we're still waiting on a number of woodworking projects from Big Lumber to help move the Sanctuary work forward Finally the statue of Bl Solanus Casey is beginning to take shape clay model is being prepared to shape the statue before a more permanent medium is cast Saturday May 16 400 4:45pm Confession 5:00pm Jim Timmerman Sunday May 17 7:10am Rosary
